title: How to Report a Phishing or Suspicious Email
text:
Q: What should I do if I receive a phishing or suspicious email?
A: If you receive a phishing or suspicious email, it's important to report it immediately to help protect yourself and others. Windows: Step 1: Do not click on any links or download attachments from the email. Step 2: Open your email client (e.g., Outlook) and select the suspicious email. Step 3: Click on the 'Report' or 'Junk' option in the toolbar. Step 4: Choose 'Phishing' as the reason for reporting. macOS: Step 1: Avoid clicking on any links or downloading attachments from the email. Step 2: Open your email application (e.g., Apple Mail) and select the suspicious email. Step 3: Click on the 'Message' menu at the top, then select 'Mark as Junk' or 'Report Junk.' Step 4: If available, choose 'Phishing' as the reason for reporting. Additionally, forward the email to the IT helpdesk at helpdesk@company.com with a brief description of why you find it suspicious. Always remember to update your passwords if you suspect any personal information may have been compromised.
